data.title: Coniagas announces $1M life offering
data.description:
• Coniagas Battery Metals (COS:CA) announced on Thursday a private placement of  17.19M units of the company at a price of $0.06 per unit for gross proceeds of up to $1.03M.
  • Each unit will consist of one common share and one common share purchase warrant.
  • Each warrant will entitle the holder to acquire an additional common share at a price of $0.08/share for a period of 5 years from the date of issuance.
  • The gross proceeds of the offering will be used to fund the advancement of its Graal property and for general corporate and working capital purposes.
  • The offering is scheduled to close on or about December 19, 2025.
  • In connection with the offering, the company has entered into a finder's fee agreement with Research Capital and agreed to pay the finder a cash commission equal to 8.0% of the gross proceeds and to issue to the finder such number of non-transferable common share purchase warrants.
  • Finder's warrant entitles the holder thereof to purchase one common share at a price of $0.06/share for a period of 5 years following the date of issuance.
  • The company also announced that it has entered into an agreement with the Finder dated November 27, 2025, pursuant to which the Finder has agreed to provide financial advisory services.
  • The company has agreed to pay the Finder a cash fee of $25,000 and issue the Finder an  0.33M common shares.

data.title: Orla Mining declares inaugural quarterly cash dividend of US$0.015 per share
data.description:
• Orla Mining (ORLA) declares $0.015/share inaugural quarterly dividend.
  • Forward yield 0.45%
  • Payable Feb. 10; to shareholders of record Jan. 12; ex-div Jan. 12.
  • The board of directors has also approved a policy under which the company intends to pay a regular quarterly dividend of US$0.015 per share, or US$0.06 per share annually.
  • "This initial return of capital represents a significant milestone in Orla's growth. Our rapid evolution from exploration to a diversified gold producer reflects both the strength of our strategy and the discipline of our execution. As construction of the South Railroad Project moves forward in 2026/2027, we will be operating in three countries across North America with a continued focus on generating sustainable value for our shareholders," said Jason Simpson, President & CEO.
  • See ORLA Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Australia's October trade surplus widens to AUD 4.39B; robust exports outpace record imports
data.description:
Australia's goods trade surplus widened to AUD 4.39 billion in October 2025, surpassing market expectations (AUD 4.2 billion) and the revised September figure (AUD 3.71 billion). This was the largest surplus since July, driven by exports rising faster than imports. 

Exports grew 3.4% month-on-month to a two-year high of AUD 45.98 billion in October 2025, after a downwardly revised 7.6% rise in September, driven by a 14.2% increase in non-monetary gold shipments to AUD 6.10 billion, following a 62.2% surge in September, amid rising prices. 

Australia’s goods imports rose 2.0% month-on-month to a fresh high of AUD 41.59 billion in October 2025, quickening from an upwardly revised 1.8% growth in the previous month amid robust domestic demand and early festive-season stocking. 

Among trading partners, exports increased to South Korea (39.1%), India (70.6%), and Japan (10.9%), while exports to the United States fell 18.5% due to the impact of newly imposed tariffs. Shipments to China, Australia’s top trading partner, also dropped by 2.2%.

The S&P/ASX 200 Index rose 0.27% to around 8,570 on Thursday, halting its two-session winning streak, as losses in gold stocks and rate-sensitive sectors offset strength in miners. The Australian dollar strengthened toward $0.661 on Thursday, extending its rally to its highest level in two months.

data.title: Sam Altman tried buying a rival to Elon Musk's SpaceX - report
data.description:
Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI (OPENAI), explored a surprise move into space sector by gathering funds to either acquire or partner with a rocket company called Stoke Space, according to a Wall Street Journal report that cites people familiar with the matter. However, after advanced talks, the deal was called off earlier this year.

Stoke Space—a rocket startup founded by former Blue Origin employees—focuses on fully reusable rockets and positions itself as an emerging competitor to Elon Musk's SpaceX (SPACE).

Sam Altman aimed to gain a controlling stake through potentially billions in equity investments; however, the discussions ended without a deal, the report added.

Altman has long been interested in building orbital data centers to power AI while minimizing environmental impact, tying into his interest in space technology beyond just launch services. The tech enthusiast is supporting another space startup, Longshot Space, that develops innovative satellite deployment technology that does not rely on traditional rocket combustion methods.

The rivalry between Sam Altman and Elon Musk has been on edge for a long time now with shared history as OpenAI (OPENAI) co-founders and competition between xAI and OpenAI. 

The proposed partnership with Stoke would place Altman in more direct competition with Musk. Besides, Merge Labs—a brain-computer interface business—rivals Elon's Neuralink, and OpenAI (OPENAI) is also building a social network that could compete with X.

data.title: Asia stocks subdued despite Wall Street gains, Japanese shares surge on tech and chipmaker gains amid rate hikes talk
data.description:
Asia stock market were largely subdued on Thursday, taking cues from a positive lead on Wall Street amid expectations that the US Federal Reserve will deliver a December rate cut, while Japanese shares rose sharply with gains in tech, chipmakers.

Traders also monitored the yen, which continued to edge higher amid speculation that the Bank of Japan could raise rates later this month.

Investors are also assessing the prospect of White House economic adviser Kevin Hassett replacing Fed Chair Jerome Powell in May, a move that could signal a push for more aggressive easing. 

Gold edged above $4,210 per ounce on Thursday, staying near a six-week high as investors grew more confident of a Federal Reserve rate cut next week. 

The benchmark KOSPI slipped 0.80% to around 4,003 on Thursday, erasing gains from earlier in the week as weakness in chip and automotive stocks weighed on the index. 

Japan (NKY:IND) rose 1.93% to above 50,100 on Thursday, extending gains from the previous session. The Japanese yen strengthened toward 155 per dollar on Thursday, trading near its highest level in more than two weeks, amid growing speculation that the Bank of Japan will raise interest rates this month.

Expectations firmed after BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda voiced confidence in Japan’s economic outlook and said the central bank would carefully weigh the pros and cons of a rate hike and act as appropriate.

Technology stocks led the advance, with notable gains from SoftBank Group (2.9%), Lasertec (1.7%), Kioxia Holdings (1.2%), Disco Corp (3.1%) and Tokyo Electron (1.7%). Robot makers also posted strong gains on renewed market interest, including Fanuc (8.8%), Yaskawa Electric (8.5%) and Nabtesco (9.3%).

China (SHCOMP) fell 0.07% to around 3860, while the Shenzhen Component rose 0.1% to 12,970 on Thursday, and the offshore yuan weakened to around 7.05 per dollar, retreating from a more than one-year high in the prior session after the People’s Bank of China set a weaker-than-expected daily fixing.

Meanwhile, investors are keenly anticipating official signals on Beijing’s economic policy and setting of growth targets for the upcoming year, with attention focused on two pivotal events: the annual Central Economic Work Conference and the December Politburo meeting.

Economists widely expect China to maintain a consistent approach to its headline economic goals. The consensus forecast suggests that Beijing will reaffirm an "around 5%" annual growth target for 2026.

Hong Kong (HSI) rose 0.15% to 25,834 in Thursday morning deals, reversing a sharp drop the previous day as gains mainly from tech and consumer stocks lifted risk appetite.

Locally, economists expect Hong Kong’s HIBOR to stay between 2-3% in the coming quarters, providing a stable backdrop for asset markets and the broader economy. 

India (SENSEX) rose 0.41% to 85,256 in volatile Thursday morning trade, halting a four-session losing streak ahead of the RBI monetary policy decision on Friday.

Australia (AS51) rose 0.27% to around 8,570 on Thursday, halting its two-session winning streak, as losses in gold stocks and rate-sensitive sectors offset strength in miners. The Australian dollar strengthened toward $0.661 on Thursday, extending its rally to its highest level in two months.

Australia's goods trade surplus widened to AUD 4.39 billion in October 2025, surpassing market expectations (AUD 4.2 billion) and the revised September figure (AUD 3.71 billion). This was the largest surplus since July, driven by exports rising faster than imports. Total exports grew 3.4% month-over-month to a two-year high of AUD 45.98 billion in October.

Gold miners Newmont and Northern Star both fell approximately 1.9%, dragging the broader gold sector down 1.3%. The financials and real estate sectors also retreated, dropping 0.4% and 2.2%, respectively, with three of the "Big Four" banks closing lower and property giant Goodman Group slipping 1.5%.

Household spending in Australia grew 1.3% mom in October 2025, picking up from an upwardly revised 0.3% gain in the prior month.

The central bank is set to meet next week, with rates already considered remaining at 3.6% after three cuts this year. 

In the U.S. on Wednesday, all three major indexes extended their recovery after softer-than-expected ADP data showed a surprise 32,000 drop in private payrolls, pushing Fed cut odds close to a certainty. The Dow rose 0.9%, the S&P 500 gained 0.4%, and the Nasdaq advanced 0.2%. Financial and defensive sectors outperformed as investors reweighted near-term policy risk.

U.S. stock futures were steady on Thursday after another winning session on Wall Street: Dow +0.11%; S&P 500 +0.06%; Nasdaq -0.05%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

data.title: Kruger Products prices senior unsecured notes
data.description:
• KP Tissue (KPT:CA)  announced on Thursday that Kruger Products has proposed a private offering to issue and sell CDN $165M principal amount of 6.250% senior unsecured notes due December 10, 2032.
  • Interest on the Notes is payable semi-annually late on June 10 and December 10 of each year.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 10, 2025.
  • National Bank Capital Markets, CIBC Capital Markets, Scotiabank, and Desjardins Capital Markets are acting as joint book-running managers for the offering.
  • In connection with the offering, Kruger Products SB will become a restricted subsidiary under KPI’s senior credit facility
  • KPI intends to use the net proceeds of the offering to fund the repayment in full of the borrowings outstanding under KPSB’s senior credit facilities and for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Paramount Skydance ups breakup fee to $5 billion in bid for Warner Bros. Discovery - Bloomberg
data.description:
• Paramount Skydance (PSKY) has more than doubled the proposed breakup fee in its offer to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) to $5 billion, as part of a sweetened proposal designed to outshine rival bids, Bloomberg News reported.
  • The sum would be paid to Warner Bros. if a deal is agreed to but not consummated, the report added.
  • It’s a sign Paramount has confidence its proposed merger would pass muster with regulators, the sources said, who asked not to be identified because the terms haven’t been made public. Paramount had earlier proposed a $2.1 billion breakup fee.
  • Shares of Paramount Skydance (PSKY) and Netflix (NFLX) fell in midday trading on Wednesday as the two media companies emerged as top bidders and are expected to butt heads for Warner Bros. Discovery's (WBD) assets.

data.title: Dollar Tree raises full-year comp outlook to 5–5.5% with multi-price strategy driving Q4 targets
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Dollar Tree, Inc. (DLTR) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Michael Creedon, CEO, described Q3 as the start of a "new era for Dollar Tree, one company, one brand, one focus." He reported "a high-quality quarter accompanied by mid-single-digit comps, above outlook earnings and strong end-of-quarter momentum heading into the holidays." He emphasized the first positive year-over-year mix shift in discretionary since Q1 2022 and highlighted "3 million more households" shopping in Q3 compared to last year, with 60% of these from higher-income brackets.
  • Creedon stated "Comparable sales increased 4.2%, a nice acceleration from the quarter-to-date trend of 3.8%." He highlighted the success of the multi-price assortment, calling it "one of the most important strategic shifts in Dollar Tree's modern history and it's working."
  • Creedon noted Halloween sales were "over $200 million, an all-time record," driven by multi-price penetration, which generated "3.5x more profit than each non-multi-price item we sold."
  • Stewart Glendinning, CFO, said "Q3 net sales increased 9.4% to $4.7 billion."
  • Glendinning added, "Q3 gross margin expanded 40 basis points to 35.8%." He attributed the EPS improvement to "freight, higher discretionary sales mix and SG&A."

Outlook

  • Glendinning provided Q4 guidance of "comps will come in between 4% and 6%, which should support net sales of $5.4 billion to $5.5 billion and adjusted EPS in the range of $2.40 to $2.60."
  • Full-year guidance was raised to "comp outlook to between 5% and 5.5% and our adjusted EPS outlook to $5.60 to $5.80."
  • Key assumptions include "net sales of approximately $19.35 billion to $19.45 billion, gross margin expansion of approximately 50 to 60 basis points" and "Dollar Tree segment SG&A deleverage of approximately 120 basis points."
  • Glendinning reiterated, "as is customary, we intend to give a detailed outlook for 2026 on our next earnings call in March."

Financial Results

  • Q3 net sales increased 9.4% to $4.7 billion, with comparable sales up 4.2%.
  • Adjusted EPS for the quarter was $1.21.
  • Gross margin expanded 40 basis points to 35.8%, attributed to "strong operational execution and cost discipline."
  • Discretionary comp increased 4.8%, consumables 3.5%.
  • Inventory was down $143 million or 5% versus prior year.
  • Free cash flow for the quarter was negative $57 million, year-to-date generated $88 million.
  • The company repurchased 4.1 million shares for $399 million in Q3, with year-to-date repurchases totaling $1.5 billion.

Q&A

  • Matthew Boss, JPMorgan: Asked about drivers of same-store sales acceleration and comp trends supporting Q4 guidance. Creedon responded, "Halloween was just a great finish to the quarter... our setup for Thanksgiving and Christmas is just fantastic."
  • Michael Lasser, UBS: Inquired about the decline in traffic and its relation to price increases. Creedon explained, "We saw the traffic decelerate in that August, September time frame. That was the peak of our restickering. Those red stickers... was the peak distraction for us."
  • John Heinbockel, Guggenheim: Questioned the divergence between traffic and units. Creedon stated, "When we take multi-price, we'll take away sections of $1.25 and so your units will naturally decline... but we believe the proof point we have from break the dollar was that you took up the price of the whole store."
  • Edward Kelly, Wells Fargo: Asked about multi-price mix next year. Creedon replied, "85% of the store is still $2 or less. So we're still early in this multi-price game."
  • Paul Lejuez, Citi: Asked about Thanksgiving weekend trends and the share of sales above $2. Creedon clarified "85% of the stores $2 or less that is on sales dollars."
  • Rupesh Parikh, Oppenheimer: Asked about elasticity and tariff relief. Creedon said, "the elasticity has really -- it's come in as we've modeled it. It's very manageable."
  • Multiple analysts focused on traffic vs. ticket mix, impact of restickering, and shrink.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a pressing and cautious tone, focusing on traffic declines, elasticity, and risk of price increases. Questions repeatedly addressed the underlying causes of negative traffic and sustainability of margin expansion.
  • Management’s tone was confident in prepared remarks, emphasizing execution and strategic shifts, but shifted to a measured and explanatory stance when addressing analyst concerns, especially on traffic and restickering. Creedon frequently referenced prior success and data-driven confidence.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s confidence in the multi-price initiative and guidance was more pronounced, while analysts were more focused on potential risks tied to customer response and cost pressures.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q2 emphasized balanced growth between traffic and ticket, with positive unit growth and discretionary comps. Q3 showed comps driven entirely by ticket, with slightly negative traffic attributed to restickering and internal disruptions.
  • Guidance was raised in Q3, both for comps and EPS, compared to Q2’s already improved outlook.
  • The multi-price strategy was central in Q3, with explicit profit and margin benefits highlighted; while Q2 focused on rollout and early customer response.
  • Analysts in Q3 were more focused on risk factors of traffic declines and cost controls than in Q2, where the tone was more positive about customer acquisition and share gains.
  • Management described a more unified brand and culture post-Family Dollar sale in Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited shrink as higher than last year, though in line with expectations, and outlined ongoing investment in asset protection and technology.
  • Analyst concerns centered on negative traffic, customer pushback on pricing, elasticity, and the impact of restickering.
  • Management indicated that restickering-related costs and disruptions are "basically done" and not expected to repeat next year.
  • SG&A pressures from payroll and restickering were addressed, with expectations for moderation in wage growth and elimination of restickering costs in 2026.

Final Takeaway

Dollar Tree leadership described Q3 as a strong validation of its focused strategy, with above-market comps, margin expansion, and clear benefits from the multi-price initiative. Management emphasized that the company is well positioned for the holiday season and confident in meeting its raised full-year guidance, highlighting operational improvements, strategic discipline, and a broadened customer base as core drivers of continued performance.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Cal-Maine strengthens Prepared Foods business, launches $15M capacity upgrade
data.description:
Cal-Maine Foods (CALM) is strengthening its prepared foods platform through new leadership appointments and targeted prepared foods production capacity expansions, it said on Wednesday.

Johnathan Zoeller has joined Cal-Maine Foods (CALM) as chief financial officer, Prepared Foods, adding valuable external experience with over 25 years as a senior financial and corporate accounting executive. He most recently served as vice president, treasurer, and head of investor relations for Westlake (WLK).

In parallel, Cal-Maine Foods is launching a new $15 million network optimization and capacity-expansion project at Echo Lake Foods, expected to add 17 million pounds of annual scrambled egg production by mid-fiscal 2027.

The project consolidates all scrambled egg manufacturing into a single, modernized facility, and expands capacity with a new production line at the facility to support both near-term demand and long-term organic growth. 

In total, these planned investments are expected to grow Cal-Maine’s prepared foods production capacity by more than 30% in the next 18-24 months.

data.title: First Majestic prices $300M offering of convertible senior notes
data.description:
• First Majestic Silver (NYSE:AG)  announced on Thursday a $300M offering of unsecured convertible senior notes due 2031.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds to repurchase a portion of its outstanding 0.375% convertible senior notes due 2027 and for general corporate purposes, including strategic opportunities.
  • The notes will bear cash interest semi-annually at 0.125% per annum. 
  • The initial conversion rate for the notes will be 44.7227 common shares of the company per $1,000 principal amount of notes, equivalent to approximately $22.36 per share.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 8, 2025.
  • Shares -1.38%.
